pattern: Because one operation instantly and permanently corrects every position sharing a chosen current value anywhere in the array, only the number of distinct current values sitting at mismatched positions ever matters.
hints: An operation is chosen by value, not by position: picking a value fixes every maximal run of that value across the whole array in one shot, wherever those runs happen to be.
Once a position is set to its target value it never needs to change again, and a position that already matches its target can simply be ignored.

Because every occurrence of a given current value gets swept up together the first time that value is chosen, the minimum number of operations is exactly the number of distinct current values that appear at positions where nums differs from target.

edge_inputs.description: the reference example needing two operations via a helpful merge
the reference example where one operation fixes two disjoint matching runs at once
the reference example needing one operation per distinct current value
arrays already equal need zero operations
a single mismatched element, smallest possible length
a single matching element, smallest possible length
every position shares the same current value but only some positions actually need to change
every position has a distinct current value and every position needs to change
values at the maximum allowed magnitude

stress.description: random values from a small alphabet so distinct-value counting is exercised heavily
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    target = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, target]

stress.description: random values across the full range at the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, target]
